metadata.summary: A collection of helpers and mock objects for unit tests and doc tests.

documentation:

# Testfixtures

[CircleCI] [Docs]

Testfixtures is a collection of helpers and mock objects that are useful when
writing automated tests in Python.

The areas of testing this package can help with are listed below:

**Comparing objects and sequences**

Better feedback when the results aren't as you expected along with
support for comparison of objects that don't normally support
comparison and comparison of deeply nested datastructures.

**Mocking out objects and methods**

Easy to use ways of stubbing out objects, classes or individual
methods. Specialised helpers and mock objects are provided, including sub-processes,
dates and times.

**Testing logging**

Helpers for capturing logging and checking what has been logged is what was expected.

**Testing stream output**

Helpers for capturing stream output, such as that from print function calls or even
stuff written directly to file descriptors, and making assertions about it.

**Testing with files and directories**

Support for creating and checking both files and directories in sandboxes
including support for other common path libraries.

**Testing exceptions and warnings**

Easy to use ways of checking that a certain exception is raised,
or a warning is issued, even down the to the parameters provided.

**Testing when using django**

Helpers for comparing instances of django models.

**Testing when using Twisted**

Helpers for making assertions about logging when using Twisted.

[circleci]: https://circleci.com/gh/simplistix/testfixtures/tree/master
[docs]: http://testfixtures.readthedocs.org/en/latest/

functions.name: testfixtures.utils.generator
documentation:
A utility function for creating a generator that will yield the
supplied arguments.

functions.name: testfixtures.utils.wrap
documentation:
A decorator that causes the supplied callables to be called before
or after the wrapped callable, as appropriate.

modules.name: testfixtures.rmtree
documentation: lamosity needed to make things reliable on Windows :-(
(borrowed from Python's test_support.py)

functions.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.tempdir
documentation:
A decorator for making a {class}`TempDirectory` available for the
duration of a test function.

All arguments and parameters are passed through to the
{class}`TempDirectory` constructor.

classes.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.create
documentation:
Create a temporary directory for this instance to use if one
has not already been created.

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.cleanup
documentation:
Delete the temporary directory and anything in it.
This {class}`TempDirectory` cannot be used again unless
{meth}`create` is called.

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.cleanup_all
documentation:
Delete all temporary directories associated with all
{class}`TempDirectory` objects.

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.listdir
documentation:
Print the contents of the specified directory.

```
param path

The path to list, which can be:

, indicating the root of the temporary
directory should be listed.

A tuple of strings, indicating that the
elements of the tuple should be used as directory
names to traverse from the root of the
temporary directory to find the directory to be
listed.

A forward-slash separated string, indicating
the directory or subdirectory that should be
traversed to from the temporary directory and
listed.

param recursive

If , the directory specified will have
its subdirectories recursively listed too.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.compare
documentation:
Compare the expected contents with the actual contents of the temporary
directory. An {class}`AssertionError` will be raised if they are not the
same.

```
param expected

A sequence of strings containing the paths
expected in the directory. These paths should
be forward-slash separated and relative to
the root of the temporary directory.

param path

The path to use as the root for the comparison,
relative to the root of the temporary directory.
This can either be:

A tuple of strings, making up the relative path.

A forward-slash separated string.

If it is not provided, the root of the temporary
directory will be used.

param files_only

If specified, directories will be excluded from
the list of actual paths used in the comparison.

param recursive

If False, only the direct contents of
the directory specified by path will be included
in the actual contents used for comparison.

param followlinks

If True, symlinks and hard links
will be followed when recursively building up
the actual list of directory contents.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.makedir
documentation:
Make an empty directory at the specified path within the
temporary directory. Any intermediate subdirectories that do
not exist will also be created.

```
param dirpath

The directory to create, which can be:

A tuple of strings.

A forward-slash separated string.

returns

The absolute path of the created directory.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.write
documentation:
Write the supplied data to a file at the specified path within
the temporary directory. Any subdirectories specified that do
not exist will also be created.

The file will always be written in binary mode. The data supplied must
either be bytes or an encoding must be supplied to convert the string
into bytes.

```
param filepath

The path to the file to create, which can be:

A tuple of strings.

A forward-slash separated string.

param data

 containing the data to be written, or a 
if encoding has been supplied.

param encoding

The encoding to be used if data is not bytes. Should
not be passed if data is already bytes.

returns

The absolute path of the file written.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.as_string
documentation:
Return the full path on disk that corresponds to the path
relative to the temporary directory that is passed in.

```
param path

The path to the file to create, which can be:

A tuple of strings.

A forward-slash separated string.

returns

A string containing the absolute path.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.as_path
documentation:
Return the {class}`~pathlib.Path` that corresponds to the path
relative to the temporary directory that is passed in.

```
param path

The path to the file to create, which can be:

A tuple of strings.

A forward-slash separated string.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.tempdirectory.TempDirectory.read
documentation:
Reads the file at the specified path within the temporary
directory.

The file is always read in binary mode. Bytes will be returned unless
an encoding is supplied, in which case a unicode string of the decoded
data will be returned.

```
param filepath

The path to the file to read, which can be:

A tuple of strings.

A forward-slash separated string.

param encoding

The encoding used to decode the data in the file.

returns

The contents of the file as a  or , if encoding
is not specified.
```

A class representing a temporary directory on disk.

```
param ignore

A sequence of strings containing regular expression
patterns that match filenames that should be
ignored by the  listing and
checking methods.

param create

If , the temporary directory will be created
as part of class instantiation.

param path

If passed, this should be a string containing an
absolute path to use as the temporary directory. When
passed,  will not create a new
directory to use.

param encoding

A default encoding to use for  and
 operations when the encoding parameter
is not passed to those methods.

param cwd

If True, set the current working directory to be that of the temporary directory
when used as a decorator or context manager.
```

functions.name: testfixtures.datetime.mock_datetime
documentation:
```
.. currentmodule:: testfixtures.datetime
```

A function that returns a mock object that can be used in place of
the {class}`datetime.datetime` class but where the return value of
{meth}`~MockDateTime.now` can be controlled.

If a single positional argument of `None` is passed, then the
queue of datetimes to be returned will be empty and you will need to
call {meth}`~MockDateTime.set` or {meth}`~MockDateTime.add` before calling
{meth}`~MockDateTime.now` or {meth}`~MockDateTime.utcnow`.

If an instance of {class}`~datetime.datetime` is passed as a single
positional argument, that will be used as the first date returned by
{meth}`~MockDateTime.now`

```
param year

An optional year used to create the first datetime returned by .

param month

An optional month used to create the first datetime returned by .

param day

An optional day used to create the first datetime returned by .

param hour

An optional hour used to create the first datetime returned by .

param minute

An optional minute used to create the first datetime returned by .

param second

An optional second used to create the first datetime returned by .

param microsecond

An optional microsecond used to create the first datetime returned by
.

param tzinfo

An optional , see .

param delta

The size of the delta to use between values returned from mocked class methods.
If not specified, it will increase by 1 with each call to .

param delta_type

The type of the delta to use between values returned from mocked class methods.
This can be any keyword parameter accepted by the  constructor.

param date_type

The type to use for the return value of the mocked class methods.
This can help with gotchas that occur when type checking is performed on values returned
by the  method.

param strict

If True, calling the mock class and any of its methods will result in an instance of
the mock being returned. If False, the default, an instance of
 will be returned instead.
```

The mock returned will behave exactly as the {class}`datetime.datetime` class
as well as being a subclass of {class}`~testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime`.

functions.name: testfixtures.datetime.mock_date
documentation:
```
.. currentmodule:: testfixtures.datetime
```

A function that returns a mock object that can be used in place of
the {class}`datetime.date` class but where the return value of
{meth}`~datetime.date.today` can be controlled.

If a single positional argument of `None` is passed, then the
queue of dates to be returned will be empty and you will need to
call {meth}`~MockDate.set` or {meth}`~MockDate.add` before calling
{meth}`~MockDate.today`.

If an instance of {class}`~datetime.date` is passed as a single
positional argument, that will be used as the first date returned by
{meth}`~datetime.date.today`

```
param year

An optional year used to create the first date returned by .

param month

An optional month used to create the first date returned by .

param day

An optional day used to create the first date returned by .

param delta

The size of the delta to use between values returned from .
If not specified, it will increase by 1 with each call to .

param delta_type

The type of the delta to use between values returned from .
This can be any keyword parameter accepted by the  constructor.

param strict

If True, calling the mock class and any of its methods will result in an instance of
the mock being returned. If False, the default, an instance of 
will be returned instead.
```

The mock returned will behave exactly as the {class}`datetime.date` class
as well as being a subclass of {class}`~testfixtures.datetime.MockDate`.

functions.name: testfixtures.datetime.mock_time
documentation:
```
.. currentmodule:: testfixtures.datetime
```

A function that returns a {class}`mock object <testfixtures.datetime.MockTime>` that can be
used in place of the {func}`time.time` function but where the return value can be
controlled.

If a single positional argument of `None` is passed, then the
queue of times to be returned will be empty and you will need to
call {meth}`~MockTime.set` or {meth}`~MockTime.add` before calling
the mock.

If an instance of {class}`~datetime.datetime` is passed as a single
positional argument, that will be used to create the first time returned.

```
param year

An optional year used to create the first time returned.

param month

An optional month used to create the first time.

param day

An optional day used to create the first time.

param hour

An optional hour used to create the first time.

param minute

An optional minute used to create the first time.

param second

An optional second used to create the first time.

param microsecond

An optional microsecond used to create the first time.

param delta

The size of the delta to use between values returned.
If not specified, it will increase by 1 with each call to the mock.

param delta_type

The type of the delta to use between values returned.
This can be any keyword parameter accepted by the  constructor.
```

The {meth}`~testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.add`, {meth}`~testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.set`
and {meth}`~testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.tick` methods on the mock can be used to
control the return values.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.add
documentation:
This will add the {class}`datetime.datetime` created from the
supplied parameters to the queue of datetimes to be returned by
{meth}`~MockDateTime.now` or {meth}`~MockDateTime.utcnow`. An instance
of {class}`~datetime.datetime` may also be passed as a single
positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.set
documentation:
This will set the {class}`datetime.datetime` created from the
supplied parameters as the next datetime to be returned by
{meth}`~MockDateTime.now` or {meth}`~MockDateTime.utcnow`, clearing out
any datetimes in the queue. An instance
of {class}`~datetime.datetime` may also be passed as a single
positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.tick
documentation:
This method should be called either with a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`
as a positional argument, or with keyword parameters that will be used
to construct a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`.

The {class}`~datetime.timedelta` will be used to advance the next datetime
to be returned by {meth}`~MockDateTime.now` or {meth}`~MockDateTime.utcnow`.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.now
documentation:
---
param tz: |-
  An optional timezone to apply to the returned time.
  If supplied, it must be an instance of a
  {class}`~datetime.tzinfo` subclass.
---

This will return the next supplied or calculated datetime from the
internal queue, rather than the actual current datetime.

If `tz` is supplied, see {ref}`timezones`.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.utcnow
documentation:
This will return the next supplied or calculated datetime from the
internal queue, rather than the actual current UTC datetime.

If you care about timezones, see {ref}`timezones`.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDateTime.date
documentation:
This will return the date component of the current mock instance,
but using the date type supplied when the mock class was created.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDate.add
documentation:
This will add the {class}`datetime.date` created from the
supplied parameters to the queue of dates to be returned by
{meth}`~MockDate.today`. An instance
of {class}`~datetime.date` may also be passed as a single
positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDate.set
documentation:
This will set the {class}`datetime.date` created from the
supplied parameters as the next date to be returned by
{meth}`~MockDate.today`, regardless of any dates in the
queue. An instance
of {class}`~datetime.date` may also be passed as a single
positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDate.tick
documentation:
This method should be called either with a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`
as a positional argument, or with keyword parameters that will be used
to construct a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`.

The {class}`~datetime.timedelta` will be used to advance the next date
to be returned by {meth}`~MockDate.today`.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockDate.today
documentation:
This will return the next supplied or calculated date from the
internal queue, rather than the actual current date.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.add
documentation:
This will add the time specified by the supplied parameters to the
queue of times to be returned by calls to the mock. The
parameters are the same as the {class}`datetime.datetime`
constructor. An instance of {class}`~datetime.datetime` may also
be passed as a single positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.set
documentation:
This will set the time specified by the supplied parameters as
the next time to be returned by a call to the mock, regardless of
any times in the queue. The parameters are the same as the
{class}`datetime.datetime` constructor. An instance of
{class}`~datetime.datetime` may also be passed as a single
positional argument.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.tick
documentation:
This method should be called either with a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`
as a positional argument, or with keyword parameters that will be used
to construct a {class}`~datetime.timedelta`.

The {class}`~datetime.timedelta` will be used to advance the next time
to be returned by a call to the mock.

methods.name: testfixtures.datetime.MockTime.__new__
documentation:
Return a {class}`float` representing the mocked current time as would normally
be returned by {func}`time.time`.

methods.name: testfixtures.tests.test_date.TestDate.test_today

classes.name: testfixtures.shouldwarn.ShouldWarn
documentation:
This context manager is used to assert that warnings are issued
within the context it is managing.

```
param expected

This should be a sequence made up of one or more elements,
each of one of the following types:

A warning class, indicating that the type
of the warnings is important but not the
parameters it is created with.

A warning instance, indicating that a
warning exactly matching the one supplied
should have been issued.

If no expected warnings are passed, you will need to inspect
the contents of the list returned by the context manager.

param order_matters

A keyword-only parameter that controls whether the order of the
captured entries is required to match those of the expected entries.
Defaults to True.

param filters

If passed, these are used to create a filter such that only warnings you
are interested in will be considered by this 
instance. The names and meanings are the same as the parameters for
.
```

classes.name: testfixtures.shouldwarn.ShouldNotWarn
documentation:
This context manager is used to assert that no warnings are issued
within the context it is managing.

methods.name: testfixtures.tests.test_datetime.TestDateTime.test_import_and_obtain_with_lists
documentation: if you have an embedded `now` as above, *and* you need to supply
a list of required datetimes, then it's often simplest just to
do a manual try-finally with a replacer:

modules.name: testfixtures.mock
documentation:
A facade for either {mod}`unittest.mock` or its [rolling backport](https://mock.readthedocs.io), if it is
installed, with a preference for the latter as it may well have newer functionality
and bugfixes.

The facade also contains any bugfixes that are critical to the operation of
functionality provided by testfixtures.

classes.name: testfixtures.sybil.FileParser
documentation:
A [Sybil](http://sybil.readthedocs.io) parser that
parses certain ReST sections to read and write files in the
configured {class}`~testfixtures.TempDirectory`.

```
param name

This is the name of the  to use
in the Sybil test namespace.
```

functions.name: testfixtures.shouldraise.ShouldAssert
documentation:
A context manager to check that an {class}`AssertionError`
is raised and its text is as expected.

functions.name: testfixtures.replace.replace
documentation:
A decorator to replace a target object for the duration of a test
function.

functions.name: testfixtures.replace.replace_in_environ
documentation: This context manager provides a quick way to use {meth}`Replacer.in_environ`.

functions.name: testfixtures.replace.replace_on_class
documentation: This context manager provides a quick way to use {meth}`Replacer.on_class`.

functions.name: testfixtures.replace.replace_in_module
documentation: This context manager provides a quick way to use {meth}`Replacer.in_module`.

classes.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.__call__
documentation: Replace the specified target with the supplied replacement.

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.replace
documentation: Replace the specified target with the supplied replacement.

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.in_environ
documentation:
This method provides a convenient way of ensuring an environment variable
in {any}`os.environ` is set to a particular value.

If you wish to ensure that an environment variable is *not* present,
then use {any}`not_there` as the `replacement`.

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.on_class
documentation:
This method provides a convenient way to replace methods, static methods and class
methods on their classes.

If the attribute being replaced has a `__name__` that differs from the attribute
name on the class, such as that returned by poorly implemented decorators, then
`name` must be used to provide the correct name.

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.in_module
documentation:
This method provides a convenient way to replace targets that are module globals,
particularly functions or other objects with a `__name__` attribute.

If an object has been imported into a module other than the one where it has been
defined, then `module` should be used to specify the module where you would
like the replacement to occur.

methods.name: testfixtures.replace.Replacer.restore
documentation:
Restore all the original objects that have been replaced by
calls to the {meth}`replace` method of this {class}`Replacer`.

These are used to manage the mocking out of objects so that units
of code can be tested without having to rely on their normal
dependencies.

classes.name: testfixtures.replace.Replace
documentation: A context manager that uses a {class}`Replacer` to replace a single target.

classes.name: testfixtures.outputcapture.OutputCapture

methods.name: testfixtures.outputcapture.OutputCapture.compare
documentation:
Compare the captured output to that expected. If the output is
not the same, an {class}`AssertionError` will be raised.

```
param expected

A string containing the expected combined output
of stdout and stderr.

param stdout

A string containing the expected output to stdout.

param stderr

A string containing the expected output to stderr.
```

A context manager for capturing output to the
{any}`sys.stdout` and {any}`sys.stderr` streams.

```
param separate

If True, stdout and stderr will be captured
separately and their expected values must be passed to
.

param fd

If True, the underlying file descriptors will be captured,
rather than just the attributes on . This allows
you to capture things like subprocesses that write directly
to the file descriptors, but is more invasive, so only use it
when you need it.

param strip_whitespace

When True, which is the default, leading and training whitespace
is trimmed from both the expected and actual values when comparing.
```

:::{note}
If `separate` is passed as `True`,
{attr}`OutputCapture.captured` will be an empty string.
:::

modules.name: testfixtures.compat
documentation: compatibility module for different python versions

functions.name: testfixtures.django.compare_model
documentation:
Returns an informative string describing the differences between the two
supplied Django model instances. The way in which this comparison is
performed can be controlled using the following parameters:

```
param ignore_fields

A sequence of fields to ignore during comparison, most commonly
set to ['id']. By default, no fields are ignored.

param non_editable_fields

If , then fields with editable=False will be included in the
comparison. By default, these fields are ignored.
```

functions.name: testfixtures.django.compare
documentation:
This is identical to {func}`~testfixtures.compare`, but with `ignore=True`
automatically set to make comparing django {class}`~django.db.models.Model`
instances easier.

modules.name: testfixtures.twisted
documentation: Tools for helping to test Twisted applications.

classes.name: testfixtures.twisted.LogCapture

methods.name: testfixtures.twisted.LogCapture.check
documentation:
Check captured events against those supplied. Please see the `fields` parameter
to the constructor to see how "actual" events are built.

```
param order_matters

This defaults to True. If False, the order of expected logging versus
actual logging will be ignored.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.twisted.LogCapture.check_failure_text
documentation:
Check the string representation of an attribute of a logged `Failure` is as expected.

```
param expected

The expected string representation.

param index

The index into  where the failure should have been logged.

param attribute

The attribute of the failure of which to find the string representation.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.twisted.LogCapture.raise_logged_failure
documentation:
A debugging tool that raises the first failure encountered in captured logging.

```
param start_index

The index into  from where to start looking for failures.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.twisted.LogCapture.make
documentation:
Instantiate, install and add a cleanup for a {class}`LogCapture`.

```
param testcase

This must be an instance of .

param kw

Any other parameters are passed directly to the  constructor.

return

The  instantiated by this method.
```

A helper for capturing stuff logged using Twisted's loggers.

```
param fields

A sequence of field names that  will use to build
"actual" events to compare against the expected events passed in.
If items are strings, they will be treated as keys info the Twisted logging event.
If they are callable, they will be called with the event as their only parameter.
If only one field is specified, "actual" events will just be that one field;
otherwise they will be a tuple of the specified fields.
```

classes.name: testfixtures.popen.PopenBehaviour
documentation:
An object representing the behaviour of a {class}`MockPopen` when
simulating a particular command.

classes.name: testfixtures.popen.MockPopenInstance

methods.name: testfixtures.popen.MockPopenInstance.__init__
documentation: These are not types as instantiation of this class is an internal implementation detail.
A mock process as returned by {class}`MockPopen`.

classes.name: testfixtures.popen.MockPopen

methods.name: testfixtures.popen.MockPopen.set_command
documentation:
Set the behaviour of this mock when it is used to simulate the
specified command.

```
param command

A  representing the command to be simulated.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.popen.MockPopen.set_default
documentation:
Set the behaviour of this mock when it is used to simulate commands
that have no explicit behavior specified using
{meth}`~MockPopen.set_command`.

A specialised mock for testing use of {class}`subprocess.Popen`.
An instance of this class can be used in place of the
{class}`subprocess.Popen` and is often inserted where it's needed using
{func}`unittest.mock.patch` or a {class}`~testfixtures.Replacer`.

functions.name: testfixtures.logcapture.log_capture
documentation:
A decorator for making a {class}`LogCapture` installed and
available for the duration of a test function.

```
param names

An optional sequence of names specifying the loggers
to be captured. If not specified, the root logger
will be captured.
```

Keyword parameters other than `install` may also be supplied and will be
passed on to the {class}`LogCapture` constructor.

classes.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.clear
documentation: Clear any entries that have been captured.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.mark_all_checked
documentation:
Mark all captured events as checked.
This should be called if you have made assertions about logging
other than through {class}`LogCapture` methods.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.ensure_checked
documentation:
Ensure every entry logged above the specified `level` has been checked.
Raises an {class}`AssertionError` if this is not the case.

```
param level

the logging level, defaults to .
```

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.emit
documentation: Record the {class}`~logging.LogRecord`.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.install
documentation:
Install this {class}`LogCapture` into the Python logging
framework for the named loggers.

This will remove any existing handlers for those loggers and
drop their level to that specified on this {class}`LogCapture` in order
to capture all logging.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.uninstall
documentation:
Un-install this {class}`LogCapture` from the Python logging
framework for the named loggers.

This will re-instate any existing handlers for those loggers
that were removed during installation and restore their level
that prior to installation.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.actual
documentation:
The sequence of actual records logged, having had their attributes
extracted as specified by the `attributes` parameter to the
{class}`LogCapture` constructor.

This can be useful for making more complex assertions about logged
records. The actual records logged can also be inspected by using the
{attr}`records` attribute.

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.check
documentation:
This will compare the captured entries with the expected
entries provided and raise an {class}`AssertionError` if they
do not match.

```
param expected

A sequence of entries of the structure specified by the attributes
passed to the constructor.
```

methods.name: testfixtures.logcapture.LogCapture.check_present
documentation:
This will check if the captured entries contain all of the expected
entries provided and raise an {class}`AssertionError` if not.
This will ignore entries that have been captured but that do not
match those in `expected`.

```
param expected

A sequence of entries of the structure specified by the attributes
passed to the constructor.

param order_matters

A keyword-only parameter that controls whether the order of the
captured entries is required to match those of the expected entries.
Defaults to True.
```

These are used to capture entries logged to the Python logging
framework and make assertions about what was logged.

```
param names

A string (or tuple of strings) containing the dotted name(s)
of loggers to capture. By default, the root logger is
captured.

param install

If , the  will be
installed as part of its instantiation.

param propagate

If specified, any captured loggers will have their
 attribute set to the supplied value. This can
be used to prevent propagation from a child logger to a
parent logger that has configured handlers.

param attributes

The sequence of attribute names to return for each record or a callable
that extracts a row from a record.

If a sequence of attribute names, those attributes will be taken from the
. If an attribute is callable, the value
used will be the result of calling it. If an attribute is missing,
None will be used in its place.

If a callable, it will be called with the 
and the value returned will be used as the row.

param recursive_check

If True, log messages will be compared recursively by
.

param ensure_checks_above

The log level above which checks must be made for logged events.
See .
```

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.diff
documentation:
A shorthand function that uses {mod}`difflib` to return a
string representing the differences between the two string
arguments.

Most useful when comparing multi-line strings.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_simple
documentation: Returns a very simple textual difference between the two supplied objects.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_object
documentation:
Compare the two supplied objects based on their type and attributes.

```
param ignore_attributes

Either a sequence of strings containing attribute names to be ignored
when comparing or a mapping of type to sequence of strings containing
attribute names to be ignored when comparing that type.

This may be specified as either a parameter to this function or in the
context. If specified in both, they will both apply with precedence
given to whatever is specified is specified as a parameter.
If specified as a parameter to this function, it may only be a list of
strings.
```

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_exception
documentation:
Compare the two supplied exceptions based on their message, type and
attributes.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_with_type
documentation:
Return a textual description of the difference between two objects
including information about their types.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_sequence
documentation:
Returns a textual description of the differences between the two
supplied sequences.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_generator
documentation:
Returns a textual description of the differences between the two
supplied generators.

This is done by first unwinding each of the generators supplied
into tuples and then passing those tuples to
{func}`compare_sequence`.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_tuple
documentation:
Returns a textual difference between two tuples or
{func}`collections.namedtuple` instances.

The presence of a `_fields` attribute on a tuple is used to
decide whether or not it is a {func}`~collections.namedtuple`.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_dict
documentation:
Returns a textual description of the differences between the two
supplied dictionaries.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_set
documentation:
Returns a textual description of the differences between the two
supplied sets.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_text
documentation:
Returns an informative string describing the differences between the two
supplied strings. The way in which this comparison is performed
can be controlled using the following parameters:

```
param blanklines

If , then when comparing multi-line
strings, any blank lines in either argument
will be ignored.

param trailing_whitespace

If , then when comparing
multi-line strings, trailing
whilespace on lines will be ignored.

param show_whitespace

If , then whitespace characters in
multi-line strings will be replaced with their
representations.
```

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare_exception_group
documentation: Compare the two supplied exception groups

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.register
documentation:
Register the supplied comparer for the specified type.
This registration is global and will be in effect from the point
this function is called until the end of the current process.

functions.name: testfixtures.comparison.compare
documentation:
Compare two objects, raising an {class}`AssertionError` if they are not
the same. The {class}`AssertionError` raised will attempt to provide
descriptions of the differences found.

The two objects to compare can be passed either positionally or using
explicit keyword arguments named `x` and `y`, or `expected` and
`actual`, or a mixture of these.

```
param prefix

If provided, in the event of an 
being raised, the prefix supplied will be prepended to the
message in the . This may be a
callable, in which case it will only be resolved if needed.

param suffix

If provided, in the event of an 
being raised, the suffix supplied will be appended to the
message in the . This may be a
callable, in which case it will only be resolved if needed.

param x_label

If provided, in the event of an 
being raised, the object passed as the first positional
argument, or x keyword argument, will be labelled
with this string in the message in the
.

param y_label

If provided, in the event of an 
being raised, the object passed as the second positional
argument, or y keyword argument, will be labelled
with this string in the message in the
.

param raises

If False, the message that would be raised in the
 will be returned instead of the
exception being raised.

param recursive

If True, when a difference is found in a
nested data structure, attempt to highlight the location
of the difference.

param strict

If True, objects will only compare equal if they are
of the same type as well as being equal.

param ignore_eq

If True, object equality, which relies on __eq__
being correctly implemented, will not be used.
Instead, comparers will be looked up and used
and, if no suitable comparer is found, objects will
be considered equal if their hash is equal.

param comparers

If supplied, should be a dictionary mapping
types to comparer functions for those types. These will
be added to the comparer registry for the duration
of this call.
```

Any other keyword parameters supplied will be passed to the functions
that end up doing the comparison. See the
{mod}`API documentation below <testfixtures.comparison>`
for details of these.

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.CompareContext
documentation:
Stores the context of the current comparison in process during a call to
{func}`testfixtures.compare`.

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.StatefulComparison
documentation: A base class for stateful comparison objects.

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.Comparison
documentation:
These are used when you need to compare an object's type, a subset of its attributes
or make equality checks with objects that do not natively support comparison.

```
param object_or_type

The object or class from which to create the
.

param attribute_dict

An optional dictionary containing attributes
to place on the .

param partial

If true, only the specified attributes will be checked and any extra attributes
of the object being compared with will be ignored.

param attributes

Any other keyword parameters passed will placed
as attributes on the .
```

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.SequenceComparison
documentation:
An object that can be used in comparisons of expected and actual
sequences.

```
param expected

The items expected to be in the sequence.

param ordered

If True, then the items are expected to be in the order specified.
If False, they may be in any order.
Defaults to True.

param partial

If True, then any keys not expected will be ignored.
Defaults to False.

param recursive

If a difference is found, recursively compare the item where
the difference was found to highlight exactly what was different.
Defaults to False.
```

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.Subset
documentation:
A shortcut for {class}`SequenceComparison` that checks if the
specified items are present in the sequence.

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.Permutation
documentation:
A shortcut for {class}`SequenceComparison` that checks if the set of items
in the sequence is as expected, but without checking ordering.

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.MappingComparison
documentation:
An object that can be used in comparisons of expected and actual
mappings.

```
param expected_mapping

The mapping that should be matched expressed as either a sequence of
(key, value) tuples or a mapping.

param expected_items

The items that should be matched.

param ordered

If True, then the keys in the mapping are expected to be in the order specified.
Defaults to False.

param partial

If True, then any keys not expected will be ignored.
Defaults to False.

param recursive

If a difference is found, recursively compare the value where
the difference was found to highlight exactly what was different.
Defaults to False.
```

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.StringComparison
documentation:
An object that can be used in comparisons of expected and actual
strings where the string expected matches a pattern rather than a
specific concrete string.

```
param regex_source

A string containing the source for a regular
expression that will be used whenever this
 is compared with
any  instance.

param flags

Flags passed to .

param flag_names

See the .
```

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.RoundComparison
documentation:
An object that can be used in comparisons of expected and actual
numerics to a specified precision.

```
param value

numeric to be compared.

param precision

Number of decimal places to round to in order
to perform the comparison.
```

classes.name: testfixtures.comparison.RangeComparison
documentation:
An object that can be used in comparisons of orderable types to
check that a value specified within the given range.

```
param lower_bound

the inclusive lower bound for the acceptable range.

param upper_bound

the inclusive upper bound for the acceptable range.
```
